qa_pairs.question: Where can I find additional information on how to log in and transfer files with Globus?
qa_pairs.answer: You can find more information on the "How To Log In and Transfer Files with Globus" page on the Globus.org site.

qa_pairs.question: What transfer options are available in Globus's 'Transfer & Sync Options'?
qa_pairs.answer: In the 'Transfer & Sync Options,' you can choose to sync only new or changed files, delete files on destinations that do not exist in the source, preserve source file modification times, verify file integrity (which is on by default), and encrypt transfers.

qa_pairs.question: Is file integrity verification automatically enabled for Globus transfers?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, file integrity verification is on by default for Globus transfers.

qa_pairs.question: When should I use the encryption option for Globus transfers?
qa_pairs.answer: The encryption option should only be used for sensitive data because it significantly reduces transfer performance.

qa_pairs.question: What is the name of the desktop client Globus provides for personal computers?
qa_pairs.answer: Globus provides a desktop client called Globus Connect Personal for easy file transfers to and from personal computers.

qa_pairs.question: Which operating systems are supported by Globus Connect Personal?
qa_pairs.answer: Globus Connect Personal supports Windows, MacOS X, and Linux operating systems.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find setup instructions for Globus Connect Personal on various operating systems?
qa_pairs.answer: Links to setup instructions for Globus Connect Personal on various operating systems, including command-line setup for Linux, are available on the Globus Connect Personal page.

qa_pairs.question: How do I configure accessible directories and permissions when running Globus Connect Personal from the command line on Linux?
qa_pairs.answer: A FAQ on the Globus site describes how to configure accessible directories and their permissions when running Globus Connect Personal from the command line on Linux.

qa_pairs.question: What are the steps to install Globus Connect Personal?
qa_pairs.answer: To install Globus Connect Personal, first log into the Alliance Globus portal. From the 'File Manager' screen, click the 'Collections' icon on the left, then 'Get Globus Connect Personal' on the top right. Click the download link for your operating system, install the software, and you should then be able to access the endpoint.

qa_pairs.question: What is the format for a Globus Connect Personal endpoint name?
qa_pairs.answer: The full endpoint name for Globus Connect Personal is `[your username]#[name you give setup]`, for example, `smith#WorkPC`.

qa_pairs.question: What do I need to do to run Globus Connect Personal after it's installed for file transfers?
qa_pairs.answer: After installation, which is a one-time setup, you simply need to make sure the Globus Connect Personal program is running and that the endpoint isn't paused to initiate file transfers.

qa_pairs.question: What happens if the Globus Connect Personal program is closed during a file transfer?
qa_pairs.answer: If the Globus Connect Personal program closes during a file transfer to or from your endpoint, the transfer will stop. To restart it, you just need to re-open the program.

qa_pairs.question: Is it possible to transfer files directly between two personal Globus Connect Personal endpoints by default?
qa_pairs.answer: No, transferring between two personal endpoints is not enabled by default, even though you can create multiple personal endpoints.

qa_pairs.question: Who should I contact if I need to enable transfers between two personal Globus Connect Personal endpoints?
qa_pairs.answer: If you need to enable transfers between two personal Globus Connect Personal endpoints, please contact globus@tech.alliancecan.ca to set up a Globus Plus account.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find specific how-to guides for Globus Connect Personal on Mac OS X, Windows, and Linux?
qa_pairs.answer: You can find specific how-to guides for Globus Connect Personal on Mac OS X, Windows, and Linux on the Globus.org how-to pages.

qa_pairs.question: What is the primary purpose of Globus sharing?
qa_pairs.answer: Globus sharing makes collaboration easy by allowing colleagues to access files stored on your account on an Alliance cluster, even if they don't have an account on that specific system.

qa_pairs.question: With whom can I share files using Globus sharing?
qa_pairs.answer: Files can be shared with any user, anywhere in the world, who possesses a Globus account.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find instructions on how to share data using Globus?
qa_pairs.answer: You can find instructions on how to share data using Globus on the 'How To Share Data Using Globus' page.

qa_pairs.question: Is Globus sharing enabled for the Trillium system?
qa_pairs.answer: No, Globus sharing is not enabled for the Trillium system.

qa_pairs.question: For which directories is Globus sharing enabled on general-purpose clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: On general-purpose clusters, Globus sharing is enabled for `/home` (except on Rorqual) and available on demand for `/project`. It is generally not enabled for `/scratch` (except on Narval).

qa_pairs.question: Is Globus sharing enabled for the `/home` directory on the Rorqual cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: No, Globus sharing is enabled for the `/home` directory on general-purpose clusters, but Rorqual is an exception where it is not.

qa_pairs.question: Is Globus sharing enabled for the `/scratch` directory on Narval?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, Globus sharing is enabled for the `/scratch` directory on Narval, which is an exception among general-purpose clusters.

qa_pairs.question: How is Globus sharing handled for the `/project` directory on general-purpose clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: Globus sharing for the `/project` directory on general-purpose clusters is available on demand.
